message:
feat(tracker): add file change tracking with agent attribution

Implement a comprehensive file change tracking system that captures
filesystem modifications and attributes them to the agents that were
active when the changes occurred.

New types and functions in tracker.go:

FileState - Captures file metadata (ModTime, Size) for comparison
FileChangeType - Enumeration: FileAdded, FileModified, FileDeleted
FileChange - Represents a single change with before/after state
SnapshotOptions - Configuration for directory scanning (ignore paths)
DefaultSnapshotOptions - Conservative defaults that skip .git

Core operations:
- SnapshotDirectory: Walks a directory tree and captures file metadata
  into a map keyed by absolute path. Supports configurable ignore rules.
- DetectFileChanges: Compares two snapshots and returns the delta,
  detecting additions, modifications, and deletions.

Storage layer:
- RecordedFileChange: Wraps FileChange with timestamp, session, and
  agent attribution metadata
- FileChangeStore: Thread-safe bounded buffer (default 500 entries)
  with Add, Since, and All methods for querying
- GlobalFileChanges: Package-level singleton for cross-component access

Integration in send.go:
- Before sending prompts, capture a baseline snapshot of the project
  directory via SnapshotDirectory
- Track which agents (pane titles) received the prompt
- After a configurable delay (5 seconds), re-snapshot and record any
  changes via RecordFileChanges
- This enables "which agent modified which files" analysis

The delay-based approach is best-effort and accounts for the time
agents need to process prompts and write files. Changes are recorded
asynchronously to avoid blocking the send command.

Also reformatted ChangeType and CoalescedChange field alignment for
consistency and added ChangeFileChange to the ChangeType enum.
